mission.title: Install and Configure MongoDB Community Operator
mission.description: The MongoDB Community Operator allows you to deploy and manage MongoDB Community instances on Kubernetes. It simplifies the management of MongoDB clusters through Kubernetes-native APIs. However, note that this operator is deprecated, and users are encouraged to migrate to the new MongoDB Kubernetes Operator.

steps.title: Add the MongoDB Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the MongoDB Community Operator Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add mongodb https://mongodb.github.io/mongodb-kubernetes-operator

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official MongoDB Helm repository.
> Updates the Helm repository to ensure you have the latest charts.

steps.title: Install the MongoDB Community Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the MongoDB Community Operator in the specified namespace.

```bash

kubectl create namespace mongodb-operator

helm install mongodb-operator mongodb/mongodb-kubernetes-operator --namespace mongodb-operator --version 0.13.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the MongoDB Operator.
> Installs the MongoDB Operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Create a MongoDB custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a custom resource definition (CRD) for MongoDB, which defines the desired state of your MongoDB deployment.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: mongodb.com/v1
kind: MongoDB
metadata:
  name: my-mongo
  namespace: mongodb-operator
spec:
  members: 3
  type: ReplicaSet
  version: 5.0.5
EOF

```

> Creates a MongoDB instance with 3 members.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: mongodb.com/v1
kind: MongoDB
metadata:
  name: my-mongo
  namespace: mongodb-operator
spec:
  members: 3
  type: ReplicaSet
  version: 5.0.5
EOF

steps.title: Verify the MongoDB deployment
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the MongoDB deployment to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb-operator

kubectl get pods -n mongodb-operator

```

> Lists the MongoDB instances in the specified namespace.
> Lists the pods to verify they are running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the MongoDB Community Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the MongoDB Community Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all MongoDB instances and associated data.

```bash

helm uninstall mongodb-operator --namespace mongodb-operator

kubectl delete namespace mongodb-operator

```

> Uninstalls the MongoDB Operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the MongoDB Community Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your MongoDB data. This step will upgrade the operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb-operator -o json | jq '.items[] | {name: .metadata.name, spec: .spec}' > backup-mongodb.json

helm upgrade mongodb-operator mongodb/mongodb-kubernetes-operator --namespace mongodb-operator --version 0.13.0

kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb-operator

```

> Backs up the current MongoDB configuration.
> Upgrades the MongoDB Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the MongoDB instances are still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb-operator -o json | jq '.items[] | {name: .metadata.name, spec: .spec}' > backup-mongodb.json

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create MongoDB resource'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the custom resource definition is not registered correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the MongoDB CRD is installed by running 'kubectl get crd | grep mongodb'.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the MongoDB configuration is incorrect or if there are insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n mongodb-operator' for details.

troubleshooting.title: MongoDB instance not starting
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources allocated to the MongoDB pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource requests/limits in the MongoDB custom resource definition.

troubleshooting.title: Helm install fails with 'chart not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Helm repository may not be added or updated correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'helm repo update' to refresh the repository.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for MongoDB 5.0.5, improved handling of custom resource updates.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.12.0, ensure to check the compatibility of your MongoDB configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see the MongoDB instance listed with 'kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb-operator' showing the status as 'Ready'.
codeSnippets:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: mongodb.com/v1
kind: MongoDB
metadata:
  name: my-mongo
  namespace: mongodb-operator
spec:
  members: 3
  type: ReplicaSet
  version: 5.0.5
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources.
